articles.title: Pistons Host Cavaliers to Open Eastern Conference Second Round
articles.original_title: Detroit hosts Cleveland to start second round
articles.summary: The Detroit Pistons (60‑22) will open the Eastern Conference second round at home against the Cleveland Cavaliers (52‑30) on Tuesday at 7 p.m. EDT. Detroit enters the series as the top seed, while Cleveland is the fourth seed. The betting line favors Detroit by three points with an over/under of 213.5.
key_points: Pistons hold the best record in the East (60‑22) and have home‑court advantage.
Cavaliers are the fourth seed (52‑30) and are three-point underdogs.
Game time: Tuesday, 7 p.m. EDT; over/under set at 213.5 points.

articles.title: Thunder Host Lakers in Western Conference Second Round Opener
articles.original_title: Oklahoma City hosts Los Angeles to begin second round
articles.summary: The Oklahoma City Thunder (64‑18) will open the Western Conference second round at home against the Los Angeles Lakers (53‑29) on Tuesday at 8:30 p.m. EDT. Oklahoma City, the top seed, is a 16‑point favorite with an over/under of 213.5.
key_points: Thunder hold the league's best record (64‑18) and enjoy home‑court advantage.
Lakers are the fourth seed (53‑29) and face a 16‑point spread.
Game time: Tuesday, 8:30 p.m. EDT; over/under set at 213.5 points.

articles.title: Pistons and Cavaliers Set to Meet in Eastern Conference Semifinals
articles.original_title: Pistons and Cavaliers advance in NBA play-offs
articles.summary: Both the Detroit Pistons and the Cleveland Cavaliers have advanced past the first round of the NBA playoffs and will clash in a best‑of‑seven series in the Eastern Conference semifinals. The matchup follows each team's successful opening series.
key_points: Pistons and Cavaliers each won their first‑round series.
The series will be a best‑of‑seven format.
It determines the Eastern Conference finalist.

articles.title: Jarrett Allen Posts 22‑Point, 19‑Rebound Game 7 to Propel Cavs Past Raptors
articles.original_title: In his first Game 7, Jarrett Allen delivers a 22-point, 19-rebound night to help Cavs advance
articles.summary: In his first career Game 7, Cavs center Jarrett Allen delivered 22 points and 19 rebounds, leading Cleveland to a 114‑102 victory over Toronto. His performance made him the second player in franchise history with at least 20 points and 15 rebounds in a Game 7.
key_points: Allen recorded 22 points and 19 rebounds in the win.
Cavaliers defeated the Raptors 114‑102 to advance.
He became the second player in Lakers franchise history with a 20‑point, 15‑rebound Game 7.

key_points: Brown expressed displeasure with the referees after the loss.
He shared his thoughts live on the Twitch platform.
The comments came in the aftermath of the Celtics’ Game 7 exit.

key_points: Doncic's health is evaluated each week to decide on his playing status.
Coaches and medical staff are maintaining flexibility in his schedule.
The week-to-week approach leaves his return date uncertain.

key_points: Doncic is progressing slowly through rehab protocols.
The team is prioritizing long‑term health over a rushed return.
Updates will be provided as his condition improves.

articles.title: Austin Reaves Sends Special Message to LeBron James After Lakers Series Win
articles.original_title: Austin Reaves Shared Special Message With LeBron James After Lakers Series Win - Lakers Nation
articles.summary: Following the Lakers' series victory, guard Austin Reaves posted a heartfelt message to teammate LeBron James, praising his leadership and expressing gratitude for the win. The post highlighted the bond between the players and the team's momentum heading into the next round.
key_points: Reaves publicly thanked LeBron for his mentorship during the postseason.
The message was shared on social media shortly after the series-clinching game.
The gesture underscored the team's unity as they prepare for the next playoff round.

key_points: Dončić's injury has been re‑evaluated, confirming he will not play in the next game.

The Lakers gain a strategic advantage as they prepare for the matchup.

articles.title: LeBron James Thanks RJ Barrett and Raptors After Advancing to Second Round
articles.original_title: Why LeBron James Thanked RJ Barrett & Raptors After Lakers Advanced To Second Round - Lakers Nation
articles.summary: After the Lakers secured a series win, LeBron James publicly expressed appreciation for RJ Barrett and the Toronto Raptors, acknowledging their role in the team's progression to the second round. The thank‑you highlighted sportsmanship and mutual respect between the franchises.
key_points: James highlighted Barrett's performance and the Raptors' competitiveness.
The gratitude was shared via a post/interview following the series victory.
The acknowledgment emphasized the Lakers' focus on respect as they move forward.

articles.title: Hopeful Outlook for Lakers and Luka Dončić Ahead of Thunder Series
articles.original_title: Lakers, Luka Doncic Given Hopeful Note Ahead of Thunder Series - heavy.com
articles.summary: Heavy.com offered an optimistic perspective on the upcoming Thunder series, noting that both the Lakers and Dallas star Luka Dončić are on positive recovery trajectories. The article suggested that the health of key players could lead to an exciting and competitive postseason.
key_points: Both teams are hopeful that their stars will be healthy for the series.
The piece highlighted recent medical updates and training reports.
A healthy Dončić and a fully fit Lakers roster could produce high‑level competition.
